name: shaughv-tasks
description: Emmett Shaughnessy's standalone Codex task + workplace-memory skill bundle. The seven SHAUGHV tasks-* skills (tasks-start / tasks-create / tasks-update / tasks-remove plus the tasks-management, tasks-memory, and tasks-boards reference skills): stand up a self-contained .tasks/ task board + workplace memory in any repo — dated milestones with derived progress that tasks join via (ms #id), per-task handoff files with default-on verification checklists (hard, waivable completion gates with recorded waivers), owners and prerequisites, a gitignored secure/ tier for secrets, ask-once git tracking (share the board with your team through git, or keep it local; remembered in config.json) — with a live localhost dashboard (zero-dependency Node server, milestone rail, identity-verified ports so multiple boards can run on one machine), a tiered offline-capable asset loader, and opt-in board-maintenance hooks. Built for operator + agent collaboration: tasks, milestones, and memory persist across sessions, operators, and agents — resume exactly where anyone left off.

interface.shortDescription: Emmett's standalone task + workplace-memory skill bundle.
interface.longDescription: Run /tasks-start to stand up a self-contained .tasks/ task board and workplace memory in any repo, with a live localhost dashboard (milestone rail included), persistent cross-session memory, and resume-where-you-left-off — asking once whether the board is git-shared with your team or kept local. /tasks-create is the guided way to add a milestone, task, or subtask with a default-on verification checklist; /tasks-update syncs and triages tasks (including at-risk milestones) and fills memory gaps; /tasks-remove folds the memory back into the repo. Secrets never go in tasks or memory — a gitignored .tasks/secure/ holds them. Skills-only — this bundle ships no MCP servers (the board is a local Node HTTP server the skill launches, not an MCP server). Codex installs from the tracked, self-contained package under plugins/shaughv-tasks/, regenerated from repo root by build-codex-plugin.ps1.
defaultPrompt: Run /tasks-start to set up my task board and workplace memory.
Use /tasks-create to add a milestone, task, or subtask with a verification checklist.
Use /tasks-update to sync and triage my tasks and fill memory gaps.
Use /tasks-remove to fold my tasks memory back into the repo.
